items.title: 'How many beers have you had?': Flood evacuees claim racial profiling

children.content: Flood evacuees from remote Northern Territory communities say they are being "treated like prisoners" by security guards working at their emergency accommodation centre.

items.title: Paramedics unable to help bleeding mother for almost an hour, inquest hears

children.content: A paramedic tells an inquest she decided to wait for police back-up before attending to a woman who was actively harming herself in a Northern Territory domestic violence shelter.

image.altText: The exterior of the Darwin Local Court.

items.title: Police allege trio used edged weapons, slingshot in 'violent incident'

children.content: A man remains in a critical condition in hospital, and three others have been charged by police, after a "violent" altercation in the remote Northern Territory community of Ngukurr.

image.altText: An aerial photo of houses in a remote community

items.title: Remote residents 'cooking from the inside' sue NT government landlord

children.content: Residents of the Central Australian community of Papunya say the scorching hot outback summers are killing their people in unsafe housing. Now they’re suing the Northern Territory government to try to force change.

gallery.altText: A drone shot of a small remote community, which features a small number of homes built on the red dirt landscape.
A woman in a brightly coloured short with grey hair sits in a darkened room holding a white metal cup.
An Indigenous woman and two children sitting on a mattress that rests on the flood inside a home.

items.title: China suddenly grants new beef licences to Australia

children.content: China has granted beef export approval to several new Australian facilities, just months after introducing a range of trade measures to protect its domestic cattle industry.

image.altText: Chef cooking beef with China and Australian flags in the background

items.title: Inquest hears of mother's final moments before death in Darwin safe house

children.content: An inquest has heard harrowing details of the final moments of a 33-year-old mother who bled to death
after harming herself inside a Darwin domestic violence shelter.

items.title: Kakadu National Park airport thrown lifeline to avoid bulldozers

children.content: Jabiru Airport had been slated for demolition in October this year, but after months of uncertainty, a new operator has emerged to take over.

items.title: Police charge man, 20, with murder over 26-year-old's death in Arnhem Land

children.content: Northern Territory police have charged a 20-year-old man with the murder of a 26-year-old man in Milingimbi last week.

image.altText: NT Police insignia on mud-splattered car door.

items.title: NT ranked last for illicit tobacco laws in new report

children.content: Smoking is one of the leading causes of death in the Northern Territory. Now, the jurisdiction has been ranked the weakest in the country for how it tackles cheap, illicit tobacco.

children.content: Anthony Albanese secures four additional cargoes of diesel for Australia via BP, Viva Energy and Export Finance Australia. Follow live.

items.title: 'Back of every parent's mind': Brain experts, clubs welcome NRL changes

children.content: Junior rugby league clubs and neuroscientists have welcomed changes to address growing concerns around injuries but say more should be done to protect children.

items.title: Dispute leaves devastated home owners stuck in 'limbo'

children.content: Five years after signing contracts and paying their deposits, families say their lives have been put on hold as they wait to move into their "forever homes" in Newcastle.

items.title: Carpooling an 'effective solution' to fuel prices, so why aren't more doing it?

children.content: Despite fuel prices soaring in March, the number of vehicles travelling along a key carpooling lane in Sydney increased by just 1 per cent. A transport expert says more needs to be done to encourage people to share their commutes.

image.altText: Woman leaning on car.

items.title: Chocolate-box violin inspires teen to retrace relative's Anzac footsteps

children.content: Ernest Sidney Pilcher died of his wounds in France on June 25, 1918. This Anzac Day, inspired by a remarkable family relic, his great-great-great-nephew will deliver a eulogy at his gravesite.

items.title: US authorities probe possible ChatGPT involvement in university shooting

items.title: What 100 years of Australian soil can tell us about our history and future

children.content: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.
